title: Lilith Rei LCL Sea question
wikitext:
[]: Lilith-Rei/LCL Sea question             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 19, 2006 3:06 pm *inhales deeply* Okay, this question of mine is gonna be weirdly worded because I don't really know how else to ask it but here goes: you know how in EoE shogoki's inside Lilith-Rei? Well, what puzzles me is HOW is Shinji in the Sea of LCL when Lilith-Rei's up there in space, as is Eva 01? I mean, obviously, he's still in Lilith-Rei, as Eva 01 bursts out of Lilith's eye towards the film's end, but are we just to assume that Lilith-Rei is connected to the LCL Sea, since she's all, uh, giant and such? Also, despite Rei III having fused with Lilith, is she still herself? I mean, how was she able to still be herself in the LCL Sea scene? Maybe her quantam magic at work again? One last thing: when Kaworu, Rei, and Yui are talking to Shinji as Eva 01 reverses everything that Lilith has done, they're obivously not inside Lilith-Rei as that's happening, right? :shock: Ugh. ....Damn it, I need to go lie down now. @_@;

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 19, 2006 5:44 pm It's symbolic.

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Thu Oct 19, 2006 7:01 pm The only one I can attempt to answer is the Rei-Lillith one. My impression was that all Rei's had combined together to form a complete being. (Remember the 3 Rei's after Gendo was chomped?) In essence, Rei IS Lillith. Only a small part of Lillith was left to "welcome back" Rei. So, basically, it was all Rei. Lillith was nothing but a giant sack of marshmellowy mesntruation without good ol Ayanami. I cant really answer the other ones, because I really dont know.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 19, 2006 10:31 pm Nukie wrote: Lillith was nothing but a giant sack of marshmallow and menstruation without her soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 20, 2006 1:09 pm I was wondering (and still kinda am) about why/HOW the 3 Reis appeared. I always just assumed that maybe it meant Rei 3 remembered everything, but that scene is already so trippy what with Gendou getting killed despite the fact Yui-sama's out in space... gag. Yes, fan theories are a very happy thing. ;

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 20, 2006 1:22 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I was wondering (and still kinda am) about why/HOW the 3 Reis appeared.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Fri Oct 20, 2006 1:41 pm that was Rei 3 in ep 1? O_O explaination now.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 20, 2006 2:07 pm InvaderRen wrote: that was Rei 3 in ep 1? O_O explaination now.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 20, 2006 2:38 pm Contrasted with the other two Rei, Rei 3 is associated with appearing in strange places in a way that should theoretically be impossible (meltdown, EoE) and with the schoolgirl getup (e.g. EoTV) whenever she isn't buck-naked.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 20, 2006 3:15 pm It was Rei. The specific incarnation is a matter capable of question.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 20, 2006 3:51 pm But what points us to Rei 3 being the first and last, and the countless number of transition guides during 3I as opposed to something like, Rei aleph-one? In each of the appearances of this Rei outside of instrumentality or dream-world (for lack of a better word) a real Rei existed. ep1, Rei 2 was in Nerv (presumably), ep26' Rei 3 was inside Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 20, 2006 9:03 pm The explaination that Rei III was the one to appear at 1 and 26' makes a WHOLE LOT MORE sense. I was racking my brains out with that one! @_@ (Guess that means Rei III is also the one who appears out in space in 26', looking @ Yui-sama.)

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Oct 20, 2006 9:08 pm The Rei that appears in EP 1 is actually related to my theory of NERV DID WTC. I know she is because I read it on the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 20, 2006 10:45 pm Reichu wrote: ...became a demented version of her current self, ate half of Gendo, and promptly vanished, leaving behind the part she didn't like as proof that she was there...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 25, 2006 8:26 pm Split my response and subsequent responses into a separate thread, "Gendo's Mysterious Induction, or, How I Ate My Husband" .
